documentation.readme: PoshC2 is a proxy aware C2 framework used to aid penetration testers with red teaming, post-exploitation and lateral movement.
usage_sections: Consistent and Cross-Platform support using Docker.
Highly configurable payloads, including default beacon times, jitter, kill dates, user agents and more.
A large number of payloads generated out-of-the-box which are frequently updated.
Shellcode containing in-build AMSI bypass and ETW patching for a high success rate and stealth.
Auto-generated Apache Rewrite rules for use in a C2 proxy, protecting your C2 infrastructure and maintaining good

documentation.when_to_use: Use during the 'any' phase against surfaces [web] in the c2 category. Fits when the engagement needs: PoshC2 is a proxy aware C2 framework used to aid penetration testers with red teaming, post-exploitation and lateral movement.
documentation.why_to_use: Prefer this tool based on upstream description: PoshC2 is a proxy aware C2 framework used to aid penetration testers with red teaming, post-exploitation and lateral movement.
documentation.how_to_use: Run the primary entrypoint as shown in upstream docs, e.g. `./Install.sh -b <git branch> -p <Directory to clone PoshC2 to>`. Further invocations from upstream: `curl -sSL $KFIOSA_TARGET_URL | sudo bash`; `sudo ./Install.sh`. PoshC2 is a proxy aware C2 framework used to aid penetration testers with red teaming, post-exploitation and lateral movement.

name: PoshC2
summary: PoshC2 is a proxy aware C2 framework used to aid penetration testers with red teaming, post-exploitation and lateral movement.

trust.warning: Attribution / index entry only. The operator must audit the code, the README, the license, and the releases before invoking. Phase 5 — entry is auto-generated from the operator's curated fetch list; no automated enrichment yet.

use_cases: When you need c2, use PoshC2 because PoshC2 is a proxy aware C2 framework used to aid penetration testers with red teaming, post-exploitation and lateral movement.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `./Install.sh -b <git branch> -p <Directory to clone PoshC2 to>` as documented upstream for PoshC2.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `curl -sSL $KFIOSA_TARGET_URL | sudo bash` as documented upstream for PoshC2.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `sudo ./Install.sh` as documented upstream for PoshC2.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `curl -sSL $KFIOSA_TARGET_URL | sudo bash -s -- -p /root/PoshC2` as documented upstream for PoshC2.
When chaining c2 tools, pair PoshC2 with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `PoshC2 --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.
